task_context.actionable_question: Which weeks should the admin schedule check-ins or motivational nudges?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Use early_warning_week [FE] to pinpoint critical weeks. Recommend admin action timing (e.g. week 5 intervention).

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: The engagement trend shows a significant decline over the observed weeks, with a sharp drop in total clicks from a peak of 228,353 in week 3 to a trough of 2,117 in week 39. This represents a staggering decrease of 94.48% in engagement, indicating a critical need for intervention.

Insights: Overall Decline in Engagement: The total clicks decreased from 38,370 in week -2 to 2,117 in week 39, marking a decline of 36,253 clicks. This represents a 94.48% drop, highlighting a severe engagement issue. | Critical Drop Weeks Identified: Notable drop weeks include week 6 (62,604 clicks), week 11 (26,756 clicks), and week 12 (16,424 clicks), each showing significant declines from previous weeks, indicating periods of low engagement. | Peak and Trough Analysis: The peak engagement occurred in week 3 with 228,353 clicks, while the lowest engagement was recorded in week 39 with 2,117 clicks. This drastic difference signifies a loss of student interest or participation over time.

Educational implications: The significant decline in engagement suggests that students may be losing motivation or facing challenges that hinder their participation. This necessitates timely interventions to re-engage students.

Recommendations: high - Schedule check-ins or motivational nudges during weeks 6, 11, and 12. - These weeks have shown significant drops in engagement, indicating critical points where students may need additional support or encouragement.

Warnings: Trend points capped at 40 of 42 rows.

insights.title: Overall Decline in Engagement
insights.description: The total clicks decreased from 38,370 in week -2 to 2,117 in week 39, marking a decline of 36,253 clicks. This represents a 94.48% drop, highlighting a severe engagement issue.

insights.title: Critical Drop Weeks Identified
insights.description: Notable drop weeks include week 6 (62,604 clicks), week 11 (26,756 clicks), and week 12 (16,424 clicks), each showing significant declines from previous weeks, indicating periods of low engagement.

insights.description: The peak engagement occurred in week 3 with 228,353 clicks, while the lowest engagement was recorded in week 39 with 2,117 clicks. This drastic difference signifies a loss of student interest or participation over time.

educational_implications: The significant decline in engagement suggests that students may be losing motivation or facing challenges that hinder their participation. This necessitates timely interventions to re-engage students.

recommendations.action: Schedule check-ins or motivational nudges during weeks 6, 11, and 12.
recommendations.rationale: These weeks have shown significant drops in engagement, indicating critical points where students may need additional support or encouragement.

warnings: Trend points capped at 40 of 42 rows.

confidence.reason: The dataset includes a robust sample size of 1998 students across 9 assessments, providing a strong statistical basis for the analysis.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify the critical weeks in which cohort-level engagement declined.
Recommend admin action timing relative to the identified critical weeks.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use early_warning_week as the primary timing field when returned.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
